sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.125 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: (M.\,Bridson). Let$F_m$be a free group of rank$m$and let$(F_m)$be a polynomially growing automorphism of maximal degree$m-1$, which means that for some (equivalently, any) free basis$\{x_1, ,x_m\}$of$F_m$, the sequence$_i| ^n(x_i)|$grows at the rate of$n^{m-1}$, where$|g|$denotes the minimal length of$g$in the$x_i$and their inverses. [r]{a)} Is the free-by-cyclic group$F_m _ { }$virtually special?...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
